Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deposition Testimony Of Joann Blake And Danny K. Lee
Off Calendar Jun-05-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant Blue Shield's motion to compel the depositions of Joann Blake and Danny Lee is granted. As counsel for plaintiff represents the witnesses in the related action and previously stated that neither he nor his clients would appear for duplicative depositions in the present action, the service of the motion was proper. The depositions are to be completed on or before June 30, 2006 at times mutually convenient to the parties. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Prevailing party to submit form of order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Mental Examination Of Pltf
Off Calendar Jun-01-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Jennifer J. Walt for defendant and moving party Blue Shield of California
Richard M. Rogers for plaintiff and opposing party Martha E. Hubbard

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted with modification: Defendant Blue Shield of California's motion to compel a mental examination is Granted.
The Court finds good cause to order the requested examination subject to the conditions described in the reply memorandum of defendant. The examination shall take place on mutually convenient dates and is to be completed by June 15, 2006.

MODIFICATION: That the IME is limited to tests enumerated in the declaration submitted by defendant in support of the motion. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses
Off Calendar Jun-01-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer J. Walt for defendant and moving party Blue Shield of California
Richard M. Rogers for plaintiff and opposing party Martha E. Hubbard

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Blue Shield of California's motion to compel further responses to interrogatories and request for production of documents is Granted. Further responses and production of documents shall take place within 14 days of notice of this order. The Court finds that plaintiff failed to make a reasonable and good faith effort to informally resolve the matters in dispute. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $790 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Mo To Compel Deft To Provide Further Responses To Interrogs & Requests For Production Of Documents
Off Calendar Mar-24-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted in part and denied in part.
Interrogatories
9
Defendant is ordered to provide the names and addresses of the five persons laid off from the Creative Services department identified in its previous response.
10, 11
Denied. The objections, other than based on the right to privacy, are overruled and stricken. The responses are otherwise sufficient
12
Denied.
Request for production of documents
5, 6
Granted. The objections are overruled. Defendant is ordered to provide a further response that complies with CCP Section 2031.210.
7
Granted. The objections are overruled. Defendant may redact the names, addresses, or other personal identifying information of any third parties.
8
Denied. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
